message.content:
Explore the HQ project to understand the current data structures and how .harness/ files are used. Specifically find:

1. TypeScript interfaces for Feature, Project, Task, Progress - look in src/state/ and src/types/
2. How features.json and progress.txt are currently read/parsed
3. Any existing Rust code that handles file operations (read/write harness files)
4. The store.ts slices structure for state management

Report file paths and key code snippets for each finding.

content.text: I'll explore the HQ project systematically to understand the data structures and harness file handling.

input.description: Find type and state definition files

Setup Tauri 2.x with React 19', 198→ category: 'setup', 199→ priority: 'critical', 200→ status: 'done', 201→ passes: true, 202→ steps: [ 203→ 'Run bun create tauri-app with react-ts template', 204→ 'Add React 19 and PatternFly 6 dependencies', 205→ 'Add @zazcart/tools dependency', 206→ 'Configure vite.config.ts', 207→ 'Verify app runs with bun tauri dev', 208→ ], 209→ }, 210→]; 211→ 212→function App() { 213→ // Tab state management 214→ const [projects, setProjects] = useState([ 215→ createMockProject('proj-1', 'HQ Dashboard'), 216→ ]); 217→ const [activeProjectId, setActiveProjectId] = useState('proj-1'); 218→ const [showWelcome, setShowWelcome] = useState(true); 219→ 220→ // Theme state 221→ const [theme, setTheme] = useState<'light' | 'dark' | 'system'>('system'); 222→ const stats = { completed: 4, total: 10 }; // F001 + F002 + F009 + F010 223→ 224→ // Log state 225→ const [logs, setLogs] = useState(createMockLogs); 226→ 227→ const handleClearLogs = useCallback(() => { 228→ setLogs([]); 229→ }, []); 230→ 231→ // Tab handlers 232→ const handleSelectProject = useCallback((projectId: string) => { 233→ setActiveProjectId(projectId); 234→ }, []); 235→ 236→ const handleCloseProject = useCallback((projectId: string) => { 237→ setProjects((prev) => { 238→ const filtered = prev.filter((p) => p.id !== projectId); 239→ // If closing active tab, switch to previous or next 240→ if (activeProjectId === projectId && filtered.length > 0) { 241→ const idx = prev.findIndex((p) => p.id === projectId); 242→ const newActive = filtered[Math.max(0, idx - 1)]; 243→ setActiveProjectId(newActive.id); 244→ } 245→ return filtered; 246→ }); 247→ }, [activeProjectId]); 248→ 249→ const handleAddProject = useCallback(() => { 250→ const id = `proj-${Date.now()}`; 251→ const newProject = createMockProject(id, `Project ${projects.length + 1}`); 252→ setProjects((prev) => [...prev, newProject]); 253→ setActiveProjectId(id); 254→ }, [projects.length]); 255→ 256→ const handleReorderProjects = useCallback((order: string[]) => { 257→ setProjects((prev) => { 258→ const projectMap = new Map(prev.map((p) => [p.id, p])); 259→ return order.map((id) => projectMap.get(id)).filter(Boolean) as Project[]; 260→ }); 261→ }, []); 262→ 263→ // Get active project 264→ const activeProject = projects.find((p) => p.id === activeProjectId); 265→ 266→ const masthead = ( 267→ 268→ 269→ 270→ HQ 271→ 272→ 273→ 274→ 275→ ); 276→ 277→ return ( 278→ 279→ {/* Tab Bar */} 280→ 288→ 289→ {/* Welcome Alert */} 290→ {showWelcome && ( 291→ 292→ setShowWelcome(false)} />} 296→ > 297→ 298→ Manage your AI agent development sessions with visual task boards and real-time logs. 299→ 300→ 301→ 302→ )} 303→ 304→ {/* Project Content */} 305→ 306→ 307→ 308→ {activeProject ? activeProject.name : 'No Project Selected'} 309→ 310→ 311→ {activeProject ? file.content:
# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard
# Session Progress Log

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - Initial Setup
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Created project structure at ~/antonio/HQ
- Wrote HQ_SPEC.md with full system specification
- Configured .claude hooks (session-start, protect-deps, quality-check)
- Set up .harness/ with progress.txt and features.json
- Initialized Tauri 2.x project with React 19
- Installed dependencies:
  - react@19.2.3, react-dom@19.2.3
  - @patternfly/react-core@6.4.0
  - @patternfly/react-icons@6.4.0
  - @patternfly/react-styles@6.4.0
  - @zazcart/tools@0.1.0
  - @tauri-apps/cli@2.9.6, @tauri-apps/api@2.9.1
- Created basic App.tsx with PatternFly components
- Created Rust backend (src-tauri/) with Tauri 2.x setup

## Key decisions:
- Stack: Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6
- State management: @zazcart/tools
- PTY: Rust portable-pty crate (to be added)
- File watching: Rust notify crate (to be added)

## Next steps:
- F009: Configure @zazcart/tools state machine
- F010: Verify Tauri app runs (bun tauri dev)
- F001: Implement multi-project tab management

## Notes:
- PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, and 19
- PatternFly has LogViewer, Chat, and Terminal components

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F010 Complete + PatternFly 6 API Fix
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Fixed PatternFly 6 API issues in App.tsx:
  - Changed Text/TextContent to Content component
  - Changed Page header prop to masthead prop
  - Fixed Label color "gold" to "yellow"
- Created app icons using `bun tauri icon` command
- Verified TypeScript compiles without errors
- Verified Tauri app builds and runs successfully
